members.name: DiffuseTexture
members.summary: The diffuse (color) texture to use. This is the main texture you want to set in most cases.

members.name: NormalTexture
members.summary: The normal map texture to use. Only has a visible effect if [Light2D]s are affecting this [CanvasTexture]. [b]Note:[/b] Godot expects the normal map to use X+, Y+, and Z+ coordinates. See [url=http://wiki.polycount.com/wiki/Normal_Map_Technical_Details#Common_Swizzle_Coordinates]this page[/url] for a comparison of normal map coordinates expected by popular engines.

members.name: SpecularColor
members.summary: The multiplier for specular reflection colors. The [Light2D]'s color is also taken into account when determining the reflection color. Only has a visible effect if [Light2D]s are affecting this [CanvasTexture].

members.name: SpecularShininess
members.summary: The specular exponent for [Light2D] specular reflections. Higher values result in a more glossy/"wet" look, with reflections becoming more localized and less visible overall. The default value of [code]1.0[/code] disables specular reflections entirely. Only has a visible effect if [Light2D]s are affecting this [CanvasTexture].

members.name: SpecularTexture
members.summary: The specular map to use for [Light2D] specular reflections. This should be a grayscale or colored texture, with brighter areas resulting in a higher [member specular_shininess] value. Using a colored [member specular_texture] allows controlling specular shininess on a per-channel basis. Only has a visible effect if [Light2D]s are affecting this [CanvasTexture].

members.name: TextureFilter
members.summary: The texture filtering mode to use when drawing this [CanvasTexture].

members.name: TextureRepeat
members.summary: The texture repeat mode to use when drawing this [CanvasTexture].
